title: PII logged without masking
description: Personally identifiable information (email, password, SSN) is written to application logs without masking or redaction. Attackers with log access can harvest sensitive data in bulk.

remediation.description: Never log raw PII. Use a masking utility to redact sensitive fields before writing to any log output.

title: PII exposed in API response without filtering
description: Full user objects containing sensitive fields (password hash, SSN, date of birth, internal IDs) are returned in API responses without field filtering or DTO projection, exposing PII to clients.

remediation.description: Never return raw database objects in API responses. Use DTOs, serializers, or explicit field selection to exclude sensitive attributes.

title: Tracking scripts loaded before user consent
description: Third-party tracking scripts (Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, etc.) are loaded in the HTML head or at page initialization without waiting for explicit user consent, violating GDPR and ePrivacy requirements.

remediation.description: Load tracking scripts only after the user has given explicit consent. Use a consent management platform (CMP) or implement a consent gate that loads scripts dynamically.
remediation.code_example:
<!-- Set type to text/plain to prevent execution -->
<script type="text/plain" data-consent="analytics">
  (function(i,s,o,g,r,a,m){...})(window,document,'script','https://www.google-analytics.com/analytics.js','ga');
</script>

// Load only after consent
function onConsentGranted() {
  document.querySelectorAll('[data-consent="analytics"]').forEach(s => {
    s.type = 'text/javascript';
  });
}

title: Cookies set without consent verification
description: Non-essential cookies are set via document.cookie without checking whether the user has given consent. This violates GDPR and ePrivacy Directive requirements for prior informed consent on tracking cookies.

remediation.description: Implement a consent check before setting any non-essential cookie. Classify cookies into categories (essential, analytics, marketing) and only set non-essential ones after consent.

title: No data retention policy implemented on user data
description: User data collections or tables have no TTL, expiration, or scheduled cleanup mechanism. Under GDPR storage limitation principle, personal data must not be kept longer than necessary for its purpose.

remediation.description: Implement a data retention policy with automatic expiration or scheduled cleanup jobs. For MongoDB, use TTL indexes. For SQL, implement soft-delete with scheduled hard-delete jobs.

title: Sensitive financial or authentication data in application logs
description: Credit card numbers, authentication tokens, passwords, or SSNs are written to application logs. Attackers with log access can harvest credentials and financial data.

remediation.description: Implement a structured logging library with automatic PII redaction. Register sensitive field names in a deny-list and strip them before writing to any log sink.

title: Cross-border data transfer without adequate safeguards
description: Personal data is sent to external APIs or services in jurisdictions without adequate data protection, and the transfer lacks encryption or contractual safeguards (SCCs). GDPR Chapter V requires documented safeguards for international transfers.

remediation.description: Encrypt personal data before cross-border transfer. Ensure third-party processors have signed Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) with Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs). Conduct Transfer Impact Assessments (TIAs) for transfers outside the EU/EEA.

title: No data deletion endpoint (right to erasure)
description: The application provides CRUD operations for user data but lacks a DELETE endpoint or data erasure mechanism. GDPR Article 17 grants data subjects the right to have their personal data erased upon request.

remediation.description: Implement a DELETE endpoint for user data that performs cascading deletion of all associated personal data across all storage systems (database, cache, backups, logs). Support both hard-delete and anonymization strategies.
